channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: 45 sentenced to up to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Dozens of prominent activists were sentenced to up to 10 years in prison on Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a sweeping law imposed by Beijing that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's state Supreme Court on Monday weighed in on a flashpoint amid ongoing vote counting in the U.S. Senate election between Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge strikes down Wyoming abortion laws, including an explicit ban on pills to end pregnancy
item.description: A judge has struck down Wyoming’s overall ban on abortion and its first-in-the nation explicit prohibition on the use of medication to end pregnancy.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazil’s G20 summit produces a broad declaration that’s short on specifics
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The summit of the Group of 20 leading economies in Rio de Janeiro produced a joint declaration Monday that, while not totally endorsed by one of the group's members, succeeded in addressing most topics host Brazil had prioritized addressing: both ongoing major wars, a global pa
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, MAURICIO SAVARESE and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TSA intercepts loaded handgun found in flight crew member's carry-on at DC-area airport
item.description: TSA officers at Reagan National Airport in Arlington, Virginia, intercepted a loaded 9mm handgun from a flight crew member's carry-on baggage on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The older sister of Daniel Penny, the former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway, told jurors Monday that her brother was a “calm, soft-spirited person” with a reputation for honesty and integrity.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KRON4

item.title: Georgia appeals court cancels hearing in election interference case against Trump
item.description: A Georgia appeals court has canceled oral arguments that were scheduled for next month on the appeal of a lower court ruling allowing Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to continue to prosecute the election interference case she brought against President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agent says GPS data puts Georgia student Laken Riley in same area as man accused in her death
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — An FBI special agent testified Monday that electronic location data seems to place Georgia nursing student Laken Riley and the man accused of killing her in the same wooded area at the time of her death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump to attend SpaceX 'Starship' launch in Texas: Sources
item.description: Leading up to the Nov. 5 election, SpaceX CEO Elon Musk showed support for Trump while on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Yañez, NewsNation

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden seeks nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden is requesting nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton, and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About one in five Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KRON4

item.title: Scaffolding collapse in NYC sends 3 people to the hospital: FDNY
item.description: The New York Fire Department responded to a scaffolding collapse in Manhattan early Monday morning that sent three people to the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Young people, whose futures are at stake in UN climate talks, push through anger to fight for hope
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Young people who attend the United Nations climate talks have a lot to be angry about. They've lost loved ones and months of school. They've lost homes and family farms and connections to their families' native lands. They haven't lost hope, though. Not yet.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 8-year-old who raised money for mother's headstone now giving back
item.description: Emouree Johnson, 8, ran a lemonade stand earlier this year to raise money for a headstone for her mother. Now, she's raising funds to help other kids.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, ABC 4

item.title: 3 hurt when plane crashes near Southern California dragstrip hosting racing event
item.description: At least two parked vehicles on the ground were struck when the small plane crashed around near the dragstrip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How much does Instacart pay? I became a full-service shopper to find out
item.description: According to ZipRecruiter, the average pay for Instacart shoppers in Maryland is about $17 per hour. I made less than that.
item.dc:creator: Tommy Tindall, ABC 4

item.title: 1 in 5 US residents 'more likely' to move after election, according to Redfin
item.description: Of those who say they are now more likely to move, 36% say the election results are making them consider a different country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs is trying to obstruct justice from jail, prosecutors say
item.description: Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs has tried to reach out to prospective witnesses and influence public opinion from jail in a bid to affect potential jurors for his upcoming sex trafficking trial, prosecutors claimed in a court filing urging a judge to reject his latest bail request.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Netflix users report outages during Tyson-Paul boxing match
item.description: Just as thousands of Netflix users flocked to the app to stream Friday night's head-to-head boxing match between champion Mike Tyson and YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul, the stream crashed.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: What happens next in The Onion's effort to buy Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: The Onion’s winning bid for Alex Jones ’ Infowars platform is under review by a federal bankruptcy judge after Jones and his lawyers complained about how an auction was conducted.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The world's tallest teenager, 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux, plans to redshirt at Florida this season
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — The world’s tallest teenager might have to wait a year to become the world’s tallest college basketball player. Florida coach Todd Golden says Olivier Rioux, a 7-foot-9 freshman who owns a spot in the Guinness record book, is planning to redshirt.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical examiner says subway madman had drugs in system in Marine vet’s chokehold trial
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg's office's last witness against Marine vet Daniel Penny is the medical examiner who conducted Jordan Neely's autopsy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: Mike Tyson slapped Jake Paul at their final weigh-in. Here's why Tyson claims he did it
item.description: A meeting between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul briefly devolved into chaos on Thursday after Tyson slapped Paul across the face during the final weigh-in before their highly anticipated fight on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Conan O’Brien will host 2025 Oscars
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A year after turning to comedian Jimmy Kimmel to host their big show, The Academy Awards will pivot to another familiar TV funnyman — Conan O’Brien. “America demanded it and now it’s happening: Taco Bell’s new Cheesy Chalupa Supreme.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kentucky governor says investigators will determine what caused deadly Louisville factory explosion
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky's governor pledged Thursday that investigators will find out what caused a deadly explosion that ripped apart a Louisville factory and left its shellshocked neighbors demanding answers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans marks with parade the 64th anniversary of 4 little girls integrating city schools
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans marked the 64th anniversary of the day four Black 6-year-old girls integrated New Orleans schools with a parade — a celebration in stark contrast to the tensions and anger that roiled the city on Nov. 14, 1960.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN SMITH and CHEVEL JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Quincy Jones' cause of death revealed: reports
item.description: The multi-talented music titan passed away at 91 on Nov. 3 at his home in the Bel Air section of Los Angeles, while surrounded by his family.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, ABC 4
